local_dishes.name: Fish & Chips
local_dishes.description: Crispy battered fish with golden chips, served with mushy peas or tartar sauce
local_dishes.where_to_try: The Red Lion – Parliament Street, London
source_files: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Cornish Pasty
local_dishes.description: A handheld pastry filled with meat, potatoes, and vegetables — originally for miners
source_files: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Sausage Roll
local_dishes.description: Puff pastry wrapped around seasoned sausage meat
source_files: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Sunday Roast with Yorkshire Pudding
local_dishes.description: A traditional roast (often beef or chicken) served with roast potatoes, vegetables, gravy, and airy Yorkshire pudding
source_files: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Sticky Toffee Pudding
local_dishes.description: A moist sponge cake with dates, drenched in toffee sauce, served with cream or ice cream
source_files: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Victoria Sponge Cake
local_dishes.description: Light sponge cake with jam and cream layers — named after Queen Victoria
source_files: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables zipped and close on public transport. Avoid late-night Tube travel alone if unfamiliar
Remember cars drive on the left; look both ways before stepping out
UK sidewalks get slippery during wet spells — wear shoes with grip

connectivity_tips.tip: There are many SIM Card providers in the UK. I would recommend Three. The best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks. Even if you do not purchase an international roaming plan for your current India number, remember to activate roaming so that you receive messages (important for things like OTPs).

There are many SIM Card providers in the UK. I would recommend Three. The best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks. Even if you do not purchase an international roaming plan for your current India number, remember to activate roaming so that you receive messages (important for things like OTPs).
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edinburgh- All Inclusive Guide.docx.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Three UK offers best balance of data and price with free roaming in many destinations. Plans from £10–£25 with 12–50GB data
EE has strongest national coverage, especially in rural areas. Plans from £15+
Vodafone UK has good nationwide reach and is popular for international calling plans. Plans from £10–£30
Buy SIM cards at airport kiosks (Heathrow, Gatwick, Edinburgh) for convenient, quick setup or high-street stores
eSIM options available through Airalo or Holafly - installable before arrival, no physical swap. Plans from £7–£20 for 5–20GB
Three UK offers best balance of data and price with free roaming in many destinations. Plans from £10–£25 with 12–50GB data.
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: EE has strongest national coverage, especially in rural areas. Plans from £15+.
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Vodafone UK has good nationwide reach and is popular for international calling plans. Plans from £10–£30.
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Buy SIM cards at airport kiosks (Heathrow, Gatwick, Edinburgh) for convenient, quick setup or high-street stores in areas like Oxford Street, Princes Street.
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: eSIM options available through Airalo or Holafly - installable before arrival, no physical swap. Plans from £7–£20 for 5–20GB.
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn All Inclusive Guide- London and Edinburgh.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Expect 4G in urban areas and 3G/occasional dead zones in remote countryside (e.g. Cotswolds hills)
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn London - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Wi-Fi is free in most cafés, trains, and attractions — but often requires email registration
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn London - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

transport_options.description: Direct trains available between major cities like London to Edinburgh

transport_options.description: Useful for Cotswolds exploration and day trips
transport_options.cost: Approx. 2–2.5 hours by car from London to Cotswolds
